steps.title: Set up the profit equation
steps.content: Using the profit formula Profit = Revenue - Total Expenses, we can express the situation algebraically. The park earns \$15 per umbrella and has \$150 in expenses. With \$435 in profit:$$435 = 15x - 150$$where $x$ is the number of umbrellas rented.

steps.title: Solve for the number of umbrellas
steps.content: Add 150 to both sides:$$585 = 15x$$Divide both sides by 15:$$x = 39$$That matches choice D — D is correct.
